2026-04-08T07:23:40.3802571Z   └ 171 lines...
2026-04-08T07:23:40.3802752Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:34.9851479Z Now I have a clear picture. The commit adds conditional preview-version filtering to the `ResourceVersions` class generator — when the default API version is GA, preview versions are excluded; when the default is preview, all versions are included. Both `design.md` and `provisioning-library-spec.md` describe `ResourceVersions` as "GA-only" without documenting this conditional behavior.
2026-04-08T07:24:34.9855609Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:36.9368253Z ● create_issue (MCP: safeoutputs) · title: "[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional pre…
2026-04-08T07:24:36.9369632Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-04-08T07:24:36.9369899Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:42.9769842Z ● dispatch_triage (MCP: safeoutputs)
2026-04-08T07:24:42.9771167Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-04-08T07:24:42.9771414Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:47.6036934Z Filed issue **[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions**.
2026-04-08T07:24:47.6037794Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:48.6848253Z **Gap identified:** Commit #57862 added conditional preview-version filtering to the `ResourceVersions` generator — previews are excluded only when the default API version is GA, and included when the default is itself a preview. Both `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` and `docs/design.md` describe `ResourceVersions` only as "GA-only" without capturing this conditional behavior or explaining the preview-detection heuristic. The issue includes an implementation guide for updating both documents.

2026-04-08T07:24:52.6469889Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6470058Z ◆ Now I understand the change clearly:
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6470314Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6471570Z   1. **Generator change**: In `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`, a new behavior was added: when the default API version is GA (not a preview), preview API versions are excluded from the `ResourceVersions` nested class. This means generated provisioning resources will only show GA versions in their `ResourceVersions` enum when the default is GA.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6472977Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6473659Z   2. **Generated file changes**: The test project files (`ConfigurationStore.cs`, `Item.cs`, `KeyValue.cs`) were updated to reflect this - they now only show GA versions, not preview ones.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6474406Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6474886Z   3. **`Azure.Provisioning.Batch/tsp-location.yaml`**: The `tsp-location.yaml` was updated - probably to point to a GA version.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6475529Z      
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6477168Z   The documentation impact is minimal since this is primarily a generator behavior change rather than a user-facing API change. The `Azure.Provisioning.Batch` README already uses `V2025_06_01` which is a GA version, so it remains accurate. However, the CHANGELOG for `Azure.Provisioning.Batch` should probably document this generator behavior change to clarify why preview versions are no longer appearing in `ResourceVersions` enums.

2026-04-08T07:24:52.6488259Z   > **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6488603Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6489474Z   But it doesn't document the new behavior added in this commit: **when the default API version is GA, preview versions are excluded from the `ResourceVersions` nested class**.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6490495Z

2026-04-08T07:24:52.6492005Z

2026-04-08T07:24:52.6495307Z   Looking at the actual changes, I can see the filtering logic was added to `ProvisioningResourceProvider.cs`, the generated test files now exclude preview versions from `ResourceVersions`, and the batch configuration was updated with a new commit SHA. The core issue is that `design.md` describes the generator's output but completely omits this new filtering behavior when the default API version is GA—it's a direct documentation gap caused by the code change.

2026-04-08T07:24:52.6504389Z ◆ I found it! In `provisioning-library-spec.md`, there's a section on "ResourceVersions Nested Class" that says:
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6505127Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6505800Z   > Lists all supported **GA-only** API versions (no previews) in reverse chronological order
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6506235Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6507020Z   But this doesn't reflect the new behavior: the spec says "GA-only" but the implementation was just changed to: when the default API version is GA, exclude preview versions. When the default is a preview, include preview versions.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6507855Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6508151Z   Let me also check the design.md to see how it describes the ResourceVersions behavior:
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6508526Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6508682Z   From the design.md:
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6509299Z   > **`ResourceVersions`**: nested class with GA API version constants
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6509628Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6509772Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6510118Z   This is also incomplete as it doesn't mention the conditional nature of preview version filtering.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6510537Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6511538Z   So the documentation gap is clear: both `provisioning-library-spec.md` and `design.md` describe ResourceVersions as GA-only or GA-focused, but they're missing the key detail that preview version inclusion is actually conditional based on whether the default API version itself is a preview.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6512604Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6514125Z   Looking at the code more carefully, the logic filters out preview versions only when the default is GA—if the default is preview, all versions get included. The spec currently just says "GA-only (no previews)" without acknowledging this conditional behavior, so I need to update it to clarify that when the default is preview, preview versions are included alongside GA versions.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6515450Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6516084Z   Since the changes are scoped to the provisioning generator package, the documentation updates should stay within that generator's docs rather than the user-facing SDK documentation.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6516791Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6518484Z   Looking at the spec docs, both `design.md` and `provisioning-library-spec.md` describe the `ResourceVersions` behavior as GA-only, but they need to account for the new conditional logic where preview versions are included when the default API version itself is a preview. The `Azure.Provisioning.Batch` package's README and CHANGELOG also need verification to ensure they reflect the updated versioning behavior.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6520366Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6521875Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture. The commit adds conditional preview-version filtering to the `ResourceVersions` class generator — when the default API version is GA, preview versions are excluded; when the default is preview, all versions are included. Both `design.md` and `provisioning-library-spec.md` describe `ResourceVersions` as "GA-only" without documenting this conditional behavior.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6523212Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6523403Z ✓ report_intent(intent: Filing documentation gap issue)
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6523631Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6524013Z ✓ safeoutputs-create_issue(title: [Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document ..., body: ## Documentation Gap
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6524426Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6524504Z **Package:** `@azu...)
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6524639Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6524770Z ✓ safeoutputs-dispatch_triage
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6524932Z 
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6525402Z ◆ Filed issue **[Provisioning Generator] Docs: Document conditional preview API version filtering in ResourceVersions**.
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6525911Z   
2026-04-08T07:24:52.6528017Z   **Gap identified:** Commit #57862 added conditional preview-version filtering to the `ResourceVersions` generator — previews are excluded only when the default API version is GA, and included when the default is itself a preview. Both `docs/provisioning-library-spec.md` and `docs/design.md` describe `ResourceVersions` only as "GA-only" without capturing this conditional behavior or explaining the preview-detection heuristic. The issue includes an implementation guide for updating both documents.

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). You can then use azd to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\n\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Batch Account with Pool and Application\n\nThis example demonstrates how to create a Batch account with a pool and application, based on the [Azure quickstart template](https://github.com/Azure/azure-quickstart-templates/blob/master/quickstarts/microsoft.batch/batchaccount-with-storage/main.
